title: Hashtag inside-link guard has broken operator precedence
description: content.rs line 71: if !before.ends_with("href=\"") && !before.contains("") Due to && binding tighter than ||, this parses as: (!ends_with_href && !contains_open_a) || contains_close_a This means: if there is ANY anywhere earlier in the string, the hashtag will always be linked even if it is currently inside an tag. The intended logic was likely: !ends_with_href && (!contains_open_a || contains_close_a) Trigger: any HTML where a hashtag appears inside an anchor tag AND there is a prior close tag. The hashtag gets double-linked, producing malformed HTML like #tag.
close_reason: Duplicate of broadside-goj2.16 (same finding, filed at higher priority; recommend upgrading goj2.16 to P0)
